description: §41.16.8 — `selectable=@cell` on `<tableFor for=T>` where struct `T` has NO `id` field AND no `selectedBy="field"` override fires E-TABLEFOR-NO-PRIMARY-KEY. `Item` has `{ sku, name }` (no `id`); the selection surface needs a primary-key field to track selected rows. Exercises the `selectable=` selection surface, which no existing table-for case reaches. Fix: add an `id` field, OR add `selectedBy="sku"` to name the PK explicitly. (Incidental W-PROGRAM-001 + W-STDLIB-SEED-FAILCLOSED co-fire; superset match.)
rationale: §41.16.8 E-TABLEFOR-NO-PRIMARY-KEY: `selectable=@cell` requires a primary key — a field named `id` or an explicit `selectedBy=` override — so the selection surface can track which rows are selected. Absent both, the compiler cannot key the selection and rejects.
